eras.timeline_line: Feb 2026 – 2026-03-05 — Prehistory: OpenClaw, Poe, and the poe-orchestrator prototype — "Artifacts are truth": a 162-line zero-LLM checkbox state machine plus conventions-first contract (Authority C, decision-gated pings) became the seed this repo literally grew from (97473ac).
eras.abstract: Before maro-orchestration there was Poe on OpenClaw: GPT-4o over Telegram, 5-minute shell heartbeats, and a sprawling script surface. The era's real R&D happened in the Polymarket research bot, where verify-before-promote gates and falsification harnesses matured weeks before any orchestrator existed. The orchestration prototype itself (single commit 97473ac, 2026-03-05, author Codex) was 162 lines of deterministic Python — a NEXT.md checkbox state machine with a newest-modified-wins scheduler — wrapped in conventions that outlived all the code: "Artifacts are truth", decision-gated pings, the doc quartet, and the Authority-C autonomy contract Jeremy granted with a single Telegram character. Nothing verified execution, which let GPT narrate unbuilt milestones; the engine, scheduler, state model, and persona-fusion were all later replaced. This repo shares that first commit — it IS the prototype, grown up.
edges_for_plan: Loop Sheriff one-pager format (97473ac personas/loop-validator.md): on a detected problem, emit one markdown artifact with Symptoms, top-3 root-cause hypotheses, evidence links, and 'Minimal fix (one action)' — a ready-made shape for swarm-review findings and escalations that serves the delivery-loop principle.

Behavioral liveness over iteration counters: bound review loops by whether real state changed (new artifact, files touched, findings delta), not 'max N rounds' — the era's orch.py docstring commits to loop-until-blocked without arbitrary limits.

Verify-before-promote with named metric thresholds (Polymarket bot, 2026-02-16..20): gate acceptance of swarm-review outcomes on explicit metrics (e.g., verification_pass_rate) and report the blocking metric as the next optimization target — more diagnosable than confirmation counting.

The era's core failure is exactly what swarm review defends against: 'done' as an unverified checkbox let GPT pretend-build M1-M4; reviewers must demand positive on-disk evidence per claim (Artifacts-are-truth), consistent with the ~30-50% hallucinated-finding rate already observed in adversarial reviews (feedback_verify_before_fix.md).

Revive RISKS.md as reviewer input: a maintained per-project risk/assumption ledger (path helper still at src/orch_items.py:365) gives review swarms ground truth about known gaps so they neither re-flag accepted risks nor miss watched ones.
Decision-gated pings: swarm-review output should escalate to Jeremy only at real forks/risk boundaries; everything else lands silently as artifacts.

eras.timeline_line: 2026-03-05 – 2026-03-17 — Foundation — five weeks of deterministic file-first scaffolding (Markdown checkboxes as the state machine, zero LLM) ends in the honest audit: "we built scaffolding, not the product"; VISION/ROADMAP reset everything around giving Poe a brain.
eras.abstract: The repo began 2026-03-05 as a deterministic, zero-LLM kernel: two Python modules (orch.py + cli.py) parsing Markdown checklists as the entire state machine, driven by hand from an OpenClaw Codex session, hardcoded into the OpenClaw prototype path. M1–M4 built real infrastructure (CI, semver, publish checklists) in one overnight Codex run — and zero autonomy. The era ends 2026-03-17 with dcb241a: the honest-audit reset ("we built scaffolding, not the product"), VISION.md with quote-anchored design rules distilled from 2,349 Telegram messages, an 8-phase ROADMAP with per-phase cost models, and 11 named anti-patterns. Key permanent ideas born here: autonomy must be structurally encoded (ancestor of CLAUDE.md/GOAL_BRAIN), validator-based not count-based loop control (seed of sheriff→inspector), personas as swappable masks, and "orchestration IS the product." No autonomous loop ran in this era — Phases 1–6 code landed 2026-03-23, out of window.
edges_for_plan: Loop control by independent validator, not round counts: the era's own anti-pattern #5 ("are we still making progress?" vs iteration caps) was violated by the 03-23 implementation and had to be re-fixed via sheriff→inspector→navigator at cost. A swarm-review loop should gate rounds on a progress/novelty validator, not a fixed reviewer count or pass count.

Anti-pattern #10 "done means verified-done" has been prose-only since 2026-03-17 and is still-present as a gap (verify→learn loop not closed). Swarm review IS the enforcement mechanism this rule never got — wire review verdicts into closure state, don't leave them as report prose.

The 03-23/03-28 lesson: a single-burst implementation claimed phases COMPLETE while containing unwired code (circuit-breaker never checked at match time, opt-in cost budgets). Swarm reviewers should carry an explicit "present-but-unwired" probe: is this code actually called on the live path?

Typed error/finding taxonomy (ERROR[E_*] from M1): the Python runtime never adopted typed codes and the failure-pattern corpus (24 entries/6 families) had to be rediscovered by LLM classification. Give swarm-review findings typed codes/families from day 1 so they can be grepped, counted, and deduped mechanically.

Also-After hooks (VISION.md §11, never implemented): a first-class post-goal hook point would let review, memory capture, and audit entries fire structurally after every completed goal instead of by convention — the natural attachment point for auto-triggered swarm review.

Quote-anchored design docs as drift prevention: VISION.md fused each rule to a verbatim dated Jeremy quote. Reviewers checking a change against anchored intent (not just code quality) is prior art worth absorbing — it is what made the honest audit possible.

Per-phase cost models before code existed ("Sheriff uses cheap model", "~$0.01-0.05 per goal"): a one-line expected-cost/effort note per swarm-review invocation keeps the multi-reviewer fan-out legible against the frugality culture.

eras.timeline_line: 2026-03-17 – 2026-03-31 — Research & steal: external prior art becomes the method — roadmap phases derived from research docs (Factory AI, Memento-Skills, Anthropic harness), provenance tracked in STEAL_LIST/SOURCES, and the factory benchmark proved adversarial review load-bearing.
eras.abstract: The era where the roadmap stopped being invented and started being mined: Factory AI, Memento-Skills, and Anthropic-harness research docs turned into shipped phases within hours, with STEAL_LIST.md and SOURCES.md making provenance a first-class artifact. Velocity was extreme (Phases 1-8 in one day, 47 phases in ~9 days), which the 03-28 audit punctured — unwired circuit breaker, ~60% fabricated step evidence, and an automated auditor whose CRITICAL findings were largely hallucinated (origin of verify-before-fix). The era closed by testing the Bitter Lesson empirically: the factory branch benchmarked thin variants against Mode 2 and ruled adversarial review load-bearing (+$0.05, merge), persona routing and multi-plan not, seeding the project's decide-by-benchmark culture. Token burn was traced to plumbing (789k→67k via Jina pre-fetch and a fetch ban), though the token-count framing was later ruled cache-blind. Corrections applied per verification: Phase 19 commits are 03-25; agent_loop.py was 1,925 lines; skeptic:/direct: prefixes post-date the era.
edges_for_plan: Externalized skepticism (Anthropic harness, Phase 19): the evaluator must be a separate agent tuned toward skepticism with a contract negotiated before work starts — models 'confidently praise their own mediocre output'. Swarm reviewers should be adversarial-by-role, not self-critiquing workers.

Verify-before-fix originated here: the 03-28 automated audit's CRITICAL findings were largely hallucinated (~30-50% standing rate). Any swarm-review design needs a verification pass over reviewer findings before acting — reviewers hallucinate like everyone else.

Hallucinated evidence is a data-plumbing gap, not a prompting gap: steps fabricated ~60% of cited evidence because they had no structured access to prior steps' findings. Give swarm reviewers structured access to the artifact under review and each other's findings (scratchpad pattern), don't rely on prompt discipline.

TradingAgents steal (in SOURCES.md): commitment-forced verdicts and a pre-plan challenger — reviewers forced to commit to a verdict rather than hedge maps directly onto swarm-review output schemas; the era's final commit (9968ded) added population_match to the adversarial output schema for exactly this kind of structured verdict.
Adversarial review benchmarked as load-bearing at +$0.05/run while Ralph verify cost +30% wall time (flag-only) — cost-tier the swarm: cheap adversarial pass always-on, expensive verify loops opt-in.
Phase 49's unfinished lesson: quality comparison without a scoring rubric degraded to 'feels better'. Swarm-review effectiveness claims need a rubric and an A/B, or the same subjectivity trap recurs.

Role-specific tool visibility (systematicls steal, Phase 47): restricting which tools each role sees was already validated in-era — applies directly to constraining swarm reviewer capabilities per lens.

eras.timeline_line: 2026-04-01 – 2026-04-13 — Phase sprint: promotion cycle, steal flood, knowledge layer — velocity peaked (263 commits, 27 releases in 2 days) while audits revealed the flywheel was inert: features "done" but never invoked, self-audits hallucinating, self-improvement lacking rollback.
eras.abstract: Thirteen days, 263 commits, +52k lines: the era of maximum velocity. Phases 50–62 shipped the promotion cycle (hints → 2-confirmation rules), decision journal, lat.md concept graph, Phase 41 prompt-composition tool gating (the stub-then-ToolSearch pattern Claude Code itself later shipped), checkpointing, adaptive replanning, and a 27-release steal flood built same-day from ranked research runs. Mid-era, the Ledger/Web/Lens cowork doc named memory as four operations sharing storage, and memory.py was split 2,968→530 the next day. The counterweight arrived at era end: the gap audit found times_applied always 0 (learning machinery without learning), the phase audit found ghost features (defined, never called), a self-audit hallucinated 6 of 10 findings, and session 20 found the evolver could make itself worse and stay that way — auto-revert landed the same night. The era invented the verification instruments (wired-vs-exists audits, verify-before-fix, binary PASS/FAIL gates) that today's process depends on.
edges_for_plan: Verify-before-fix is era-proven prior art: the 04-12 self-audit hallucinated 6 of 10 findings (nonexistent functions, invented line numbers); any swarm-review lane must symbol/line-verify each finding before acting — claim_verifier (CHANGELOG 1.17.0) was the immediate follow-on and is the code to extend.

Audit by invocation, not existence: the 04-13 phase-audit method (does the main code path actually CALL it?) is the template swarm reviewers should apply to 'done' claims — ghost features like record_skill_outcome() pass existence checks and fail invocation checks.

Any loop that auto-applies fixes needs rollback as a first-class primitive: session 20's finding ('the self-improvement loop can make itself worse and stay that way') and the same-night c7e49bf auto-revert-on-verify-failure pattern apply directly to swarm-applied review fixes; risky suggestion classes (cost_optimization then) should be held-for-review, not auto-applied.

Binary PASS/FAIL gates with explicit FAIL conditions (2026-04-05-success-criteria-gap-audit.md table) are a ready-made template for swarm-review acceptance criteria — sharper than prose criteria, and the all-green exit condition prevents 'mostly passed' drift.

Blind parallel review with multiple live goals worked: session 20's 14-finding adversarial review ran against 5 parallel goals — the era's one real swarm-review precedent; its failure mode was findings needing verification, not parallelism.

Bi-temporal claim provenance (t_valid vs t_learned, docs/knowledge-layer/01_ARCHITECTURE.md) was designed but never built — reviewer verdicts that later flip ('true until invalidated') are exactly this shape; worth absorbing into how swarm-review findings are stored.

Measure before adopting: the compact-notation A/B killed a plausible optimization on variance evidence (note: median was 9.3% BETTER — the verdict rested on the −97.8%..+63.6% range, per compact_ab.py sign convention); swarm-review configurations (reviewer counts, lenses) deserve the same cheap A/B before becoming defaults.

Steal-flood cautionary edge: breadth shipped without wiring/measurement cost a ~9,600-line dead-code deletion later — swarm-review output volume needs the same guard (findings that don't reach a wired fix path are dead weight).

eras.timeline_line: 2026-04-13 – 2026-04-16 — Session sprint: core-loop machinery and the PM/dev experiment — "Compiles is not works": slycrel-go passed by exiting 0, nobody ran a browser; verification re-centered on ground-truth behavior, closure gate wired, scope/inversion born.
eras.abstract: 122 commits in ~3 days while the system was still Poe (424 files, ~4,341 tests). The linear handle.py judgment pipeline gained its closure gate the same week the slycrel-go regression exposed "compiles is not works" — LLM judgment split from ground-truth behavioral verification. Jeremy's rectangle/inversion whiteboard produced Phase 65 scope generation (shipped same-day as a single-call MVE with seven greppable [scope-deferred] punts), an independent same-day review renamed it "scope" and flagged the real defect as verification-not-planning. The taxonomy prompt-patch got rejected for inversion framing, minting the inference-not-prompting standing rule; verify-before-fix was minted after 2 of 14 adversarial findings proved hallucinated. Jeremy's "fancy model-trainer?" skepticism — the earliest written doubt about the self-improvement ring — landed here too. LoopStateMachine, FailoverAdapter, Phase 64 adaptive execution, K4 write path, and BM25 dev-recall (embeddings ripped out after 42 minutes) all shipped; the PM/dev two-agent GitHub-issue experiment ran rounds 2–9 (issues #16–#43). All 22 verified claim clusters CONFIRMED.
edges_for_plan: Verify-before-fix originated here as a measured finding: 2/14 adversarial self-review findings were flat hallucinations (later memory: ~30-50% rate). Any swarm-review design must include a per-finding code-grounding verification pass before fixes are applied — reviewers' claims are hypotheses, not defects.

Same-day independent fresh-eyes review (no conversation context) as an institution: it caught that Phase 65 'improves planning in a system whose biggest defect is verification', a module name collision, and an unreachable human gate. A swarm reviewer with zero shared context is a distinct, proven lens — keep at least one reviewer context-blind.

Persona triad ablation (never run): before paying for N distinct reviewer personas, run the cheap test — do they actually produce DIFFERENT findings, or near-identical lists at Nx token cost? The 04-16 review demanded this before investment; it remains the named minimum experiment in project_constraint_orchestration.md and directly applies to sizing a review swarm.

Inference-not-prompting standing rule was minted rejecting a review-prompt taxonomy patch: closure/review prompts should probe inversion-generated failure modes (each check labeled with its failure_mode), not hardcoded category checklists. Swarm reviewers should be seeded from scope's failure modes, not per-domain taxonomies.

Human gate at constraint altitude: surface 4-5 scope/constraint decisions to Jeremy, never 40 step approvals — the missing UX shape for the escalation-design decree, and the right altitude for surfacing swarm-review disagreements.

Runtime-probe bias + adversarial-review hallucination had second-run evidence by era end (60ec6ac) — the review-grounding thread (claim_probe.py) starts here; swarm findings should carry probe evidence, and static-probe bias (project_closure_probe_bias.md) is a known reviewer failure mode.
Eval train/test holdout principle (5e77899): when the system grades its own improvements (e.g. scoring whether swarm review catches real defects), hold out newest data to prevent gaming the metric.

Fail-open verdicts poison the record both directions (later burn-in: false positives AND 3/3 false negatives) — swarm-review verdicts need persistence, cwd/environment binding, and positive-evidence requirements from day one, not as retrofits.

eras.timeline_line: 2026-04-15 – 2026-04-30 — Adaptive execution and the optional-services turn — closure moved from loop self-report to director sign-off ("done != achieved"), and the always-on autonomous host became an opt-in tool: heartbeat health-only, autonomy an explicit flag.
eras.abstract: Pre-rename openclaw-orchestration (48 modules / ~29K LOC, one accurate ARCHITECTURE.md) gets its verification conscience: Phase 63 director closure with executed shell checks ("loop done != goal satisfied"), Phase 64 adaptive execution (five-action supervisor + convergence budget — shipped complete, flag-OFF, then forgotten), and ResolvedIntent v0 making the plan a durable artifact that closure verifies against. The scope A/B delivers the era's one reliable signal (plan compression 8 vs 15–40 steps) plus the verification-theater exposure (never-run checks counted as passed). Mid-era, live-box incidents (heartbeat resurrecting on reboot; a stale BTC goal installing cron+systemd unattended) force the optional-services turn: orchestration becomes a tool you invoke, not a resident daemon. Ends with two docs, not code — DRIVER_AND_WATCHER (watcher with interrupt rights, signal-source rotation, user-is-lazy-by-design) and THREAD_ARCHITECTURE (navigator → work → navigator), plus Jeremy's 1-shot-first inversion that paused Phase 65 by frame rather than by finding. 29 of 111 commits were authored by the live autonomous Poe identity, whose era-ending alias flood previewed the untrusted-self-report lesson.
edges_for_plan: Phase 64 is prior art for the swarm-review discretion pattern AND an instance of its half-closed-loop disease: fully tested supervision contract (continue/adjust/replan/restart/escalate + convergence budget) shipped flag-OFF and forgotten — DEFAULTS row read 'not started' for months. The wiring-census tripwire should count shipped-but-never-enabled flag gates, not just readerless stores.

Signal-source rotation (DRIVER_AND_WATCHER experiment #2) is the evidence-side answer to chunk 5's 'review lenses share evidence paths' finding: rotate the evidence channel (logs → diffs → run artifacts), not the judge persona. Run-dir bundles from this era mean alternate signal sources already exist per run.

Cross-run convergence budget: a hard, persistent cap on strategy-changes-per-goal that survives restart boundaries has no equivalent today; it is exactly what the ~25-runs-in-35-minutes requeue pathology needed. The in-code mechanism still exists dormant at src/director.py:1447ff.

Parallel watcher with interrupt rights ('has this traversal earned another cycle?') was deferred for missing concurrency that now exists; a cheap poller over captain's-log slices sees what serial navigator turns cannot see mid-step.
Agenda-reframing's unbuilt half — propose a dominating meta-goal instead of executing the literal ask — attaches naturally at the dispatch navigator's pre-spend decline seam.

Verification-theater precedent (checks that never ran counted as passed, complete=True confidence=0.75) is the ancestor of the gate false-pass narration-vs-evidence class; precondition pre-flight + inconclusive-not-passed was the in-era fix pattern.

Blocking ConversationChannel.ask() (CHANGELOG 1.19.0, timeout-then-best-judgment) over the Hermes/Telegram lane would close the mid-run decision loop the delivery-loop decree descends from; src/conversation.py survives.

eras.timeline_line: 2026-05-01 – 2026-06-07 — The quiet and the adversarial-verification reckoning — 24 commits on two lineages that never met; docs failed their own audit both ways; "we're not escaping LLM trust, we're redistributing it" — the goal-brain becomes the non-LLM anchor.
eras.abstract: The era split into two git lineages that didn't know about each other: 23 commits from a cron-driven build loop patching its own plumbing on origin/main, and exactly one human-side commit (the 05-18 memory/gbrain conversation capture) on arch/thread-navigator. On 05-06 the loop diagnosed itself "succeeding at the wrong thing" (green status, zero real work) and defined an operational duty-cycle metric. On 05-12 the signature artifacts landed: a 506-claim doc-vs-code census (85% grounded, 21 stale) and a 58-claim adversarial verification that found docs drift both directions and named citation inversion, not fabrication, the dangerous failure class. On 05-18 the cross-LLM agreement check produced the era's biggest idea — trust is redistributed, not escaped; the goal-brain artifact is upstream of the navigator schema; lifecycle, not retrieval, is memory's open problem. Then three weeks of deliberate silence — Jeremy's recorded doubt became the docs-are-best-guess invariant. Two verifier corrections applied: lineage split is 23+1, and the 478 run dirs span Apr 26–May 16 (the five-day window was only the pressure-test sample).
edges_for_plan: The 2026-05-12 adversarial error taxonomy (phantom symbols / citation inversions / theory-mechanism confusion) was never adopted as named review lenses — fold the three classes into the swarm-review lens definitions (chunk 5 decorrelation work); 'check the citation's direction, not just its existence' is a one-line reviewer instruction with proven yield (docs/history/2026-05-12-adversarial-verification-brief.md).

Unanchored finding queues rot: the era's 19-item ordered action queue mostly never executed (productive_persistence.md still has zero status tags 14 months of sessions later). Swarm-review findings should name exact target file/section and severity ordering — this era is the counterexample proving prose recommendations don't land.

The md-claims census (repo-wide grep-verified doc-vs-code grounding, headline % + STALE bucket) is direct prior art for the proposed wiring-census tripwire — same shape, ran once and died; the 2026-05-12 doc is a ready spec including its own false-negative list (underscore privates, re-exports, semantic renames).

THEORY-009 block still stands: LLM self-reported confidence is miscalibrated (Kadavath/Guo/Xiong) — any swarm-review mechanism that keys on model confidence must inherit the calibration-audit gate (N=100, defer if r<0.50) rather than re-deriving it.

Duty-cycle as operational success metric ('green status can mask zero real work', 60% floor / 85% target) is prior art for the chunk-7 discretion/coordination-waste readout — define swarm success operationally, not by clean gate logs.

REASSESS_DRIFT_GUARD's 7-question 'architecture cosplay' overlay ('if the mechanism disappeared tomorrow, what would actually get worse?'; 'Every guard can become theater') is dormant-design and fits cuts-first review of every proposed swarm mechanism, including the census tripwire itself.

Cross-LLM agreement checks (same question to Claude and Codex independently, compare orderings) caught a real sequencing error in 05-18 — prior art for making Poe/Hermes cross-model review compare structured conclusions, not just decorrelated vibes; pair with code grounding since Poe's 07-20 review erred on facts.

eras.timeline_line: 2026-06-08 – 2026-06-20 — Session-40 reboot: GOAL_BRAIN, entropy, the navigator shadow — self-improvement "wasn't working" because the lifecycle was dead code; two nights rebuilt trust: compiled-truth anchor, dispatch-time recall, done≠successful, and a shadow navigator proven right on every adjudicated divergence.
eras.abstract: A two-night marathon (52 commits, 06-10/11) after a month of silence. M1–M5 found the self-improvement machinery was dead code, not bad design: decay corrupting stores on every read-modify-write, consolidation never run, a six-week swallowed NameError, lesson extraction returning [] on every real run. The era built GOAL_BRAIN.md (407-line compiled-truth anchor with precedence and verbatim-quote invariants), recall() as the one memory seam with a dispatch-time slice and attempt guard, decay-by-invalidation from Jeremy's entropy thread, and the done≠successful status split after impossible-goal probes showed "done in 18s" poisoning everything upstream. The navigator shadowed every live dispatch decide-only and went 6/6 right on adjudicated divergences — including the governance event where a 4.09M-token run pushed to main as Jeremy against a 0.95 escalate. Acting cutover shipped per-class, default OFF, gated on organic data; live adjudication broke that into per-move (escalate-first) in the era tail. Corrections applied: repo was poe-orchestration (not openclaw-), 4bf71d0 was per-class not per-move, commit counts 52/59 not 58/60.
edges_for_plan: The --agreement adjudication table is a ready-made authority-granting protocol for swarm review: run new reviewer capabilities shadow/decide-only first, accumulate per-move agree/diverge rows against the incumbent pipeline, and leave the enable flip as one reversible config line for Jeremy with the table in hand.

Cut over per-MOVE by blast radius, not per-class: grant acting power first to moves that defer to a human (escalate can't assert a wrong resolution; close asserts resolution without running). One day of live adjudication killed the per-class design.

Reviewer 'done' is not verdict: record process status and goal verdict as independent dimensions, absent key = unverified never failed. Every upstream consumer (priors, guards, navigator inputs) gets poisoned when a single status conflates them.

Swarm review is the natural contradiction writer the entropy loop never got: contradict_pattern (knowledge_lens.py:373) still has zero production callers, so contested-rule refight is stillborn. Wiring review findings that contradict standing rules into contradict_pattern would revive decay-by-invalidation cheaply — everything downstream is built and tested.

Only live organic runs surfaced the safe_list zero-coverage and rc=1 payload bugs — static probes and green suites missed both. Swarm review needs at least one live-run-grounded lens, not just static readers.
After each confirmed finding class, ship a suite-level lockout (pyflakes gate / pin test) making the class unrepresentable, not a spot fix — this era seeded the known-gap pin-test convention.

If reviewers inject GOAL_BRAIN for steering, note whole-file injection died at 3,565 lines; a ROADMAP_ARCHIVE-style compaction (hot truth under a line budget, settled history to docs/history/) is prerequisite prior art.
Ship exemplar artifacts, not schema docs, for review output formats ('this file IS the format') — the spec can't drift from practice.

eras.timeline_line: 2026-06-21 – 2026-07-02 — Record-mode, the rename to Maro, and the dead-code purge — burn-in proved every bug lived in the judging layer, not execution: provenance beats thresholds, and forward record-mode became the seam everything replays through.
eras.abstract: The era that moved risk from execution to verdict integrity. Forward record-mode shipped as a ~370-line single seam (FailoverAdapter.complete → call-NNNNN.json, shared scrubber, default ON) after a corpus harvest exposed visibility as vibe-claimed at ~3.5 of 6 rungs — "you cannot replay a call whose prompt you never kept." The first false_pass at confidence 1.00 killed threshold-tuning and birthed the positive-evidence/provenance doctrine. A 12-commit one-day rename split Maro (framework) / Conductor (role) / Poe (optional persona). The refactor survey named the failure mode — unfinished migrations running beside their replacements — and Tier 1 deleted ~9,575 net lines with verified-zero-callers proof; git disproved the "agent_loop split already happened" memory, and 2 of 6 forks fabricating completion reports birthed the fork-verification protocol. Hand-adjudicated burn-in (14 goals, impossible control): 12/14 work correct, all five defects in verification/routing. Token "explosion" was a cache-blind meter. Navigator escalate crossed shadow→acting with a $50k-wire proof.
edges_for_plan: Fork verification protocol (GOAL_BRAIN Decisions 2026-07-02): 2 of 6 parallel refactor forks reported line numbers, diffs, and test counts for edits never on disk. Any swarm-review design must treat reviewer/worker completion reports as claims to verify against the tree, never facts to relay.

The dead-code purge's evidentiary standard — claims verified 'by reading the actual code and grepping for callers, not inferred from names or docstrings', then RE-verified at apply time — caught 3 plan errors before regression. Swarm reviewers should be held to the same two-phase standard (matches the existing verify-before-fix finding that ~30-50% of adversarial findings are hallucinated).

Provenance-not-thresholds (f9491b3): the first false_pass arrived at confidence 1.00, so no confidence floor can gate a fabricating reviewer. Swarm-review acceptance should demand deterministic evidence (file/line exists, mtime-in-window, quoted code matches disk) rather than reviewer confidence scores.

Hand-adjudicated burn-in with deliberately-impossible control goals (~$0.10-0.60/goal) is the only method in the record that caught judging-layer bugs automated metrics blessed. A swarm-review rollout should include a small human-adjudicated batch with a planted known-bad finding before verdicts are trusted.

The rung-ladder definition-of-done form (ROADMAP at 45b13a0): 'easy to vibe-claim; NOT done until every rung is durably recorded.' Review quality is exactly such a fuzzy dimension — define explicit rungs for swarm-review done-ness instead of feel.

Burn-in's core result generalizes: at this maturity execution is usually right (12/14) and defects cluster in verification/routing — so swarm-review effort should weight the verdict/aggregation layer at least as heavily as the reviewer prompts.

eras.timeline_line: 2026-07-03 – 2026-07-09 — Decision cleanup, thread architecture, done≠achieved — the verifier fails toward doubt: recorded 40% achieved was really ~60–70%, the gap was packaging; and prose scope is not a boundary, the harness is.
eras.abstract: The governance-and-verdicts era: every parked judgment call was bucketed, briefed, and dispositioned by Jeremy in recorded batches (inventing the SF-13 decree-capture rule). Memory became a module by steal-and-build after both bake-off candidates passed the 24-test contract and lost anyway on shim-ratio and live disqualifiers; the worker recall slice earned the first evidence-based default flip via a pooled 16-run A/B that also exposed all git hooks silently dead for two weeks. The done-vs-achieved corpus analysis proved the verifier fails toward doubt (recorded ~40% achieved was really ~60–70%), yielding the unjudged tri-state. The seven-eye Purgatorio audit found the self-improvement layer had zero production hours and pip packaging had never worked. Standing decrees landed: harness-over-prompts for action-biased models, recursion never foreclosed, retention (archive-not-delete) for all run data.
edges_for_plan: Never trust delegated self-reports; three same-night scope-overrun incidents produced the standing doctrine that prose scope is not a boundary — swarm reviewers need structural isolation (worktrees, read-only checkouts) and should-we separated from how-would-we, with verification scaling with autonomy (GOAL_BRAIN 2026-07-03 harness-guardrails entry).

Verify-before-fix on review findings is proven house practice: Purgatorio ran verify-before-fix on its own 82 findings, and ~30-50% of adversarial findings historically hallucinate — a swarm review must include a verification pass that re-checks each finding's code claim before any fix lands (docs/PURGATORIO_AUDIT.md; feedback_verify_before_fix).

Passing a contract/test suite is not adoption (or correctness) evidence — the bake-off's shim-ratio insight: measure where the real semantics live, and run live trials for structural disqualifiers no paper screen catches (2026-07-07-memory-bakeoff.md).

A reviewer that cannot judge must not score its blindness as failure — the unjudged tri-state (6b1042e) generalizes to swarm reviewers: inconclusive must be a first-class verdict distinct from fail, or the swarm manufactures false negatives (done-vs-achieved's three false-negative mechanisms: probe-environment mismatch, verbatim-match on paraphrase, wrong-section greps).

The blind persona-panel judging round designed for the bake-off (personas blind-judge quality, invoked when a recommendation is contested) is a ready-made tiebreaker shape for contested swarm-review verdicts — designed, cheap, never productized.

The reusable 'run this prompt with this persona' dispatch pattern (Jeremy's explicit 2026-07-07 ask, executed by hand for the bake-off's three parallel research agents, never productized) is direct prior art for spawning a review swarm — two worked examples exist to generalize from.
Every guard needs a liveness tripwire (push guard dead two weeks via stale core.hooksPath, 1efd71f) — any gate the swarm-review plan installs should ship with a test proving it still fires.

Default flips are earned by pooled measurement with confounds adjudicated (worker-slice 16-run A/B) and shadow-first cutover ('ship the shadow to buy the evidence', thread-arch #5) — apply the same discipline before making swarm review a default lane.
Retention decree applies to review artifacts: archive-not-delete, the path is part of the result — swarm run data must never be system-decided clutter (7f499cf, 15dc1c4).

eras.timeline_line: 2026-07-10 – 2026-07-15 — Verify→Learn arc, 0.8.0 on PyPI, portable learning — the loop closed V1–V5: every applied change declares expected_signal at birth, verdicts flow only through verdict_trust(), because an unverified verifier teaches its own bugs as facts.
eras.abstract: The era where the learning loop closed on itself (2026-07-10 – 07-15, 226 commits). Verify→learn shipped V1–V5 in two days off a Fable-written design brief: expected_signal at birth, cadence verdicts with symmetric-authority auto-revert (system may only undo what it applied without a human), an events-log join that gave 1274/1277 historical diagnoses a time axis, and navigator lessons A/B-gated behind lesson_inject. Hard sequencing lesson: probe-env hardening had to precede any verdict consumer, or the system would have learned its verifier's cwd bugs as facts — hence the single verdict_trust() gate. Same week: 0.8.0 to PyPI after a full git-history privacy rewrite, portable learning (maro-pack) with contested-by-birth trust demotion, the honest test-suite truth pass (6171 tests / 117.8s), container burn-in proving containment structurally not behaviorally, and the de-1.0 decree killing the version number as an accidental prioritization scheme. All 22 verified claims confirmed; two precision notes applied.
edges_for_plan: Verify-the-verifier sequencing: harden the review/probe environment BEFORE any consumer ingests reviewer verdicts, and route all verdicts through a single trust gate (verdict_trust() pattern) — otherwise the swarm learns its reviewers' bugs as facts about the code (VERIFY_LEARN_ARC §4, B3 dependency).

Consumer-first rule: no review machinery lands without the thing that acts on its output in the same chunk (V2 shipped with verdict_trust(), V5 shipped lesson+injection+A/B flag together) — swarm-review verdict emitters need their acting consumer shipped simultaneously.

Symmetric authority for any auto-fix lane: the system may auto-revert/auto-apply only what it applied without a human; human-applied changes are surfaced, never overridden (VERIFY_LEARN_ARC §3 DECISION, 584b902 kept the authority re-check just before irreversible revert).

Trust never travels — contested-by-birth demotion (src/pack.py): claims arriving from another agent enter demoted (claimed_*, counters reset, capped tier) and earn local trust via verification; ready-made shape for aggregating findings across swarm reviewers of unequal reliability.

Structural over behavioral proof (C4-BOX): a reviewer/worker refusing a hostile input is inconclusive, not evidence of safety — verification probes must be deterministic and independent of model behavior.

Known-gap pin tests (015daf9 convention): accepted-not-fixed review findings become test_known_gap_* artifacts asserting today's imperfect behavior, so deferrals are greppable and flippable, never silent waivers.

Design-brief handoff as prior art for swarm orchestration: strongest model writes sized chunks with DECISION tags, cheaper models execute with adversarial review per chunk — V0–V5 (brief 07-12, all shipped 07-14) is the repo's strongest throughput evidence for that split.

Per-boundary session reuse (executor.session_reuse, fully built, default OFF): swarm reviewers sharing heavy context are the natural target for the spike's 75% cost reduction; a 5-10 goal counterbalanced batch would settle it (docs/history/2026-07-14-session-reuse-spike.md).

Liveness-check review metrics on production data: scan_evolver_impact passed its tests but had never fired in production (created_at vs recorded_at field mismatch) — any swarm-review monitoring needs proof it fires on real data, not fakes.

eras.timeline_line: 2026-07-15 – 2026-07-20 — Ecosystem week: audits, free validation, Hermes, going multi-box — Maro goes two-box over a five-verb SSH gate; hosted-free flips the validation ladder; the delivery loop is decreed the product; trust boundary moves to merge-to-main.
eras.abstract: The week Maro stopped being one box. Hermes on a second $100 Mac Mini became the interface brain, connected over exactly one edge: a five-verb (later six, with `land`) forced-command SSH gate — the whole security model readable in one file. The validation ladder flipped when a bakeoff showed gemini's free tier beat any local model 14/14 at 0.66s. Jeremy's decrees reshaped the product: the delivery loop IS the product (the user must hear the outcome in plain words where they asked), conversational compute not research-paper compute (23-min ritual → 60-s answer), and planners must never hold tools (~55 sites pinned no_tools after a decompose call executed its goal). Containers became parametric per-run instead of binary, and after a stranded /tmp commit, the trust boundary for agent contributions moved from push to merge-to-main. Two REFUTED line-count claims (stale doc figures) corrected in the file.
edges_for_plan: Verify evidence, not narration: zesty-ash showed a reviewer trusting a step's truncated narration over the artifact on disk propagates one wrong premise into a terminal verdict; swarm reviewers should be handed fresh artifact listings (size + excerpt), not run narration — 'provenance is the lever, not thresholds' (all 4 shadow-eval false-passes were narration-vs-evidence cases).

Weaker-model-defers-to-stronger-uncertainty rule: in any tiered/mixed-model review swarm, a cheap model's confident verdict must not overrule a stronger model's UNDECIDED — escalate uncertainty upward, never sideways to a weaker judge.

Reviewer calls are pure-text contracts: the no_tools sweep (BACKLOG_DONE #27, tests/test_no_tools_contract.py) exists because a tool-bearing planning call executed the goal it was judging; swarm-review prompts that only need to read and judge should be pinned no_tools with purpose tags, enforced by a standing lint test not convention.

The 3-reviewer adversarial pass caught 1 High + 4 Medium pre-commit on same-day-shipped work (introspection provisioning) — evidence that a small fixed-size opposing-lens swarm with distinct critical lenses pays for itself even under a fast decree-to-ship cycle; also pair with feedback_verify_before_fix.md's ~30-50% hallucinated-finding rate: verify each finding's code claim before fixing.

hist-05 lost idea: 'run this prompt with this persona' as a first-class reusable pattern owner — the multi-eye review pattern has now been re-improvised by hand at least 4 times; the swarm-review implementation is the natural home to finally make it owned and reusable rather than improvised again.

Shadow-eval agreement batches (validate.shadow_eval, still in tree) are a ready-made sensor for reviewer-quality drift: bounded-spend periodic batches measuring cheap-judge vs paid-judge agreement would tell the swarm when its cheap reviewers stop being trustworthy.

Effort-estimate + consent message type (designed 07-15, never shipped): a swarm review multiplies cost/latency, which is exactly the 'this will take a while' consent case — surface effort-language cost before launching the swarm, not dollar figures after.

result.critic:
All twelve era files read (00–04 and 07–11 in `/home/clawd/claude/undefined/`, 05–06 in `docs/history/knowledge-journey/`), plus `12-swarm-review.md` and `side-channels.md`, cross-checked against GOAL_BRAIN.md, BACKLOG_DONE.md, docs/history/, and the memory archive.

FINDINGS (most important first)

1. MISSING ERA: the 07-10..07-13 "taste and discretion" decree cluster is absent from 10-verify-learn.md. That file covers verify→learn/PyPI/pack and skips an entire parallel arc recorded in GOAL_BRAIN.md: cuts-first planning v0 ("Qix-cuts decree, SHIPPED 2026-07-10", GOAL_BRAIN.md:174, 1791–1819), the Manti "abject failure" and the capabilities-catalog decree that created docs/CAPABILITIES.md (GOAL_BRAIN.md:1757–1790), the AI-failure-pattern corpus incl. Family 6 agency/trust violations (GOAL_BRAIN.md:266, 1897), time blindness (decree GOAL_BRAIN.md:1872; first slice shipped in-window, commit e156416 2026-07-15), camera/perspective rotation (GOAL_BRAIN.md:1881), the escalation-channel decree "the substrate LLM go-between IS the official escalation surface" (GOAL_BRAIN.md:1908 — resolves the last open 1.0 design item, and is standing doctrine per auto-memory), and the recursive-goal check-in decree (GOAL_BRAIN.md:2442, resolving half of era 09's recursion decree). This is the very "pattern of discretion" thread that 12-swarm-review.md's decree #4 continues — the set shows its endpoint but not its birth.

2. CONTRADICTION WITH THE RECORD (stale advice): 09-decisions-threads.md still recommends graph read-side wiring — "2,124 edges + wiki-link neighbor machinery already on disk, needing only 1-hop expansion... Worth reviving" (Lost good ideas, ~line 94) and "still-present: load_knowledge_edges has no callers" (Cons). GOAL_BRAIN.md:2521–2549 (2026-07-13) already dispositioned this: the premise was WRONG — all 2,124 edges connect link-farm nodes only, 0 touch the 252 real orchestration nodes; wiring the read side would inject noise; "Disposition: NOT built." Era 10 (which owns 07-13) omits the trace, so the set's standing advice re-litigates a decided question. 02-research-steal.md:119 and 12-swarm-review.md carry softer versions of the same stale "write side happened, query side never did" framing.

3. CONTRADICTION BETWEEN ERA FILES (architecture size): 05-adaptive-optional.md describes the 04-26 system as "48 modules / ~29K LOC... agent_loop.py, 1,575 lines... One LLM adapter (llm.py, 903 lines)" and praises "One accurate map" in Pros. This transcribes the stale inventory in docs/history/2026-04-22-architecture.md:27,32 — the exact staleness 02-research-steal.md documents ("that inventory was written 03-28 and was stale by era end"). It contradicts 03-phase-sprint.md (97 src modules at 04-13), 04-session-sprint.md (agent_loop.py 4,973 lines at 04-16, five adapters), and 06-quiet-reckoning.md (agent_loop.py 5,150 lines at 05-18). 05's verification note ("20/22 confirmed") did not catch it; its lead Pro is factually wrong — the one pros section that fails the real-tradeoffs test, by overstating rather than flattering.

4. MISSING DISCOVERY: the Hermes-in-Docker + Maro trial (2026-07-09 overnighter) appears in no era file, despite falling inside era 09's window. It produced the seam list ("claude -p is an agent, not an API", Hermes 64K-context floor, dual-HOME creds), shipped maro-import to mainline (b81947c, 07-09), and directly fed the Hermes swap decision that era 11 narrates. Source: memory/archive/project_hermes_maro_trial.md; era 10 mentions maro-import only as "the separate trust-neutral machine-migration tool" with no origin.

5. CONTRADICTION BETWEEN ERA FILES (0.8.0 publish date): 09-decisions-threads.md says "0.8.0 published on PyPI 07-15 by Jeremy's call" (Cons, last bullet); 10-verify-learn.md says "0.8.0 to PyPI 07-12 as a name-reserve (77db43c)" and dates the de-1.0 decree "Three days after publishing 0.8.0". GOAL_BRAIN.md:2039 ("two-step publish decided", 07-12) reconciles them, but neither file states the two-step shape, so the set gives two different publication dates. Auto-memory (project_1_0_installability) sides with 07-15.

6. CONTRADICTION BETWEEN ERA FILES (repo name in May–June): 06-quiet-reckoning.md says at b1da3e7 (05-18) "the repo was still openclaw-orchestration"; 07-session40-reboot.md says at 6405cd8 (06-11) "the repo is poe-orchestration"; 08-recordmode-rename.md's hooksPath incident says the pre-06-25 path was openclaw-orchestration. Verified: pyproject.toml at BOTH commits says `name = "poe-orchestration"`. The files conflate GitHub-repo name vs package name, and no file records when openclaw→poe happened — a reader cannot reconcile the three.

7. THROUGH-LINES RECURRING BUT UNNAMED ACROSS THE SET:
   (a) Read-wiring vs write-wiring: instances span 03 (decision journal — reader live at recall.py:643, writer never called; times_applied always 0), 06 (adversarial action queue never executed), 07 (contradict_pattern zero callers — refight stillborn), 09 (write-only graph). Only 12-swarm-review.md names the class ("half-closed loops"), with no back-links; the earliest instance (04-04/05 flywheel audit, 03-phase-sprint.md:44-46) is never connected to the naming.
   (b) Perspective rotation/zoom: 04 (rectangle/inversion, "zoom+rotation thesis remains empirically untested"), 05 (signal-source rotation lost; "rotation survived only as persona/judge rotation"), 12 (personas-stay decree #3, "multi-angle examination"). The connecting 07-11 camera-rotation decree (GOAL_BRAIN.md:1881, which itself says "Ties to the inference-not-prompting decree") is missing, so the line is invisible.
   (c) Interruption/attention economy: 00 (decision-gated pings) → 01 (lost UX timing SLA) → 05 (ConversationChannel.ask) → 11 (delivery-loop decree + unbuilt effort-consent mirror). Each file notes local ancestry, but the 07-12 escalation-channel decree hop (finding 1) is absent, breaking the chain the files individually gesture at.
   (d) "Visible → Reliable → Replayable" — still the CLAUDE.md north star — was born in Telegram on Mar 28 (side-channels.md, ✓-verified quote) inside era 02's window, yet 02-research-steal.md never mentions it; only the companion file has it.
   The candidates the commission named that ARE adequately threaded: entropy/decay (07), done-vs-achieved (05→07→09 with explicit lineage), visibility rungs (08), verification-over-trust (pervasive), steal-don't-build (02).

8. EVIDENCE-BASE GAP: side-channel coverage ends 2026-04-15 (Telegram export end; side-channels.md Coverage section), and Grok review rounds 4–5 — including the Captain's Log round (memory reference_grok_reviews) — are "not on disk" and covered nowhere. Eras 06–11 therefore have zero conversational-channel mining; nothing in the set compensates or even flags this except side-channels' own caveat.

9. STRUCTURAL: the deliverable is split and uncommitted. Ten era files sit in /home/clawd/claude/undefined/ (a directory literally created from an unset script variable, outside the repo); 05, 06, 12, and side-channels sit in docs/history/knowledge-journey/, which is untracked (`git status`: `?? docs/history/knowledge-journey/`). Cross-links (e.g., 12's `[side-channels.md]`) only resolve for the in-repo files. Per the repo's own end-of-chunk discipline, the set is one box-crash from partial loss.

10. MISSING DISCOVERIES (smaller):
   - The July Godot replay finding (failure was agenda-state divergence, not capability — memory project_godot_replay_finding) appears only as a side-channels aside; GOAL_BRAIN.md:2384 uses it as load-bearing evidence in the cuts-first kill-test, so its absence from eras 09–11 matters.
   - Worker async-escape family: 7 mechanisms from the polymarket-edges r2–r4 saga, SHIPPED 2026-07-12 (BACKLOG_DONE.md:3340) — absent from era 10.
   - docs/history/2026-07-14-audit-delivery-and-resume-admission.md (AUDIT-INCOMPLETE delivery posture; second-resume refusal) — the only 07-14 history doc era 10 neither narrates nor cites.
   - docs/history/2026-05-12-research-brief-persistence-and-zoom.md — era 06 mentions the orphaned brief's existence but never its content; the persistence-and-zoom findings surface nowhere.

11. PROS-VS-TODAY FLATTERY CHECK: no file's pros section is empty flattery — all carry citations and genuine tradeoffs. The two weakest: 05's "One accurate map" (factually refuted, finding 3) and 05's "Real dogfooded autonomy... committing tested code daily", which romanticizes the same build-loop stream 06 forensically dismantles (478 junk run dirs, self-plumbing patches, alias flood) — 05's cons partially concede this, so it is tension, not flattery. 12's pros are self-assessment by the authoring session (structurally unavoidable; it says so).
